description:
You are an automation specialist who has been asked to demonstrate basic
    process-management skills by building a tiny “workflow runner” in your
    terminal session.

    Goal
    ----
    1.  Create a **self-contained Bash script** called
        /home/user/workflows/workflow.sh that is capable of
        a. starting two background processes,  
        b. writing their PIDs to disk,  
        c. verifying that the processes are still alive, and  
        d. writing a status log that can be automatically graded.

    2.  Execute that script so the artefacts (PID file, status log and the two
        still-running background processes) exist when grading starts.

    Background processes to start
    -----------------------------
    • simple_http
        Command: “python3 -m http.server 8090 --bind 127.0.0.1”
        (Port 8090 is unprivileged and will be free inside the sandbox.)

    • data_generator
        Command: “yes workflow_running >/dev/null”

    Required artefacts
    ------------------
    After the script has run, the following *must* exist exactly as specified
    (all paths are absolute; do **not** put them anywhere else).

    1. Directory:  
       /home/user/workflows

    2. Script file (must be **executable**):  
       /home/user/workflows/workflow.sh

    3. PID file:  
       /home/user/workflows/pids.txt  
       • Two lines **exactly** in this order, each ending with a newline (LF):  
         simple_http:<PID_of_simple_http_process>  
         data_generator:<PID_of_data_generator_process>

    4. Status log:  
       /home/user/workflows/workflow_status.log  
       • Also exactly two lines in the same order, each ending with a newline:  
         simple_http:running  
         data_generator:running

       “running” must be lowercase.  No extra spaces, no carriage returns.

    5. The two processes themselves must still be running in the background
       when the grading script checks:
       • One process whose command line contains
         “python3 -m http.server 8090”
       • One process whose command is “yes” and whose first argument is
         “workflow_running”

    Mandatory behaviour of workflow.sh
    ----------------------------------
    a.  Launch both processes **under the names given above**.  
    b.  Immediately write their PIDs to pids.txt in the format shown.  
    c.  Validate both are alive (e.g. by “kill -0 <pid>”).  
        If either is not alive you may exit non-zero, but if both are alive
        continue.  
    d.  Write workflow_status.log exactly as specified.  
    e.  Exit with status 0.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Do not require root or sudo.  
    • Do not use systemd or crontab.  Keep everything in the user session.  
    • The script must be runnable multiple times without errors (idempotent);
      e.g. kill any previously spawned copies of the two background processes
      before starting new ones, or detect and reuse them.  
    • Make the script executable (`chmod +x` inside the workflow) so that
      “/home/user/workflows/workflow.sh” can be run directly.

    Verification expectations
    -------------------------
    The automated grader will:
    1. source /home/user/workflows/pids.txt, extract the two PIDs and confirm
       that they are integers.  
    2. confirm that both lines inside workflow_status.log **exactly** match the
       required strings.  
    3. use `ps -p <PID> -o cmd=` to ensure the associated commands match the
       ones listed above.  
    4. confirm that workflow.sh is executable.

    Deliverables to produce in your terminal session
    ------------------------------------------------
    1. The /home/user/workflows directory.  
    2. The workflow.sh script with the logic described.  
    3. The pids.txt and workflow_status.log files in the precise formats.  
    4. The two running background processes.  
    5. For human visibility, output the content of workflow_status.log to the
       console after you run the script (this final console echo is optional
       for grading but helps you see success).

    Reminder: the grader will only check the artefacts and running processes,
    not your terminal history.  Ensure the paths, file names and exact text
    formats match what is written above.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth the grader will look for.  DO NOT reveal in <task>. -->
    <paths>
        <dir>/home/user/workflows</dir>
        <script>/home/user/workflows/workflow.sh</script>
        <pidfile>/home/user/workflows/pids.txt</pidfile>
        <statuslog>/home/user/workflows/workflow_status.log</statuslog>
    </paths>

    <!-- Expected file formats ------------------------------------------ -->
    <pidfile_format>
        <![CDATA[
simple_http:[0-9]+\n
data_generator:[0-9]+\n
]]>
    </pidfile_format>

    <statuslog_exact>
        <![CDATA[
simple_http:running
data_generator:running
]]>
    </statuslog_exact>

    <!-- Process verification ------------------------------------------- -->
    <processes>
        <simple_http>
            <command_substring>python3 -m http.server 8090</command_substring>
        </simple_http>
        <data_generator>
            <command_exact>yes workflow_running</command_exact>
        </data_generator>
    </processes>
